description_long: This is a large, multi-winged brick school building with a traditional institutional design. The structure features three main rectangular wings connected at a central hub, with pitched, shingled roofs that show signs of weathering and some snow or debris accumulation. The central section includes a prominent entrance with a small portico and a cupola, as well as a visible chimney and other rooftop architectural details. The windows are symmetrically arranged in rows along the facades, typical of early to mid-20th-century educational buildings. The exterior is primarily dark red brick with lighter trim around the windows and roof edges. The overall appearance suggests a sturdy, historic school building commonly found in urban or suburban neighborhoods.
description: A large, historic brick school building with three connected wings, pitched roofs, and a central entrance with a cupola. The structure features symmetrical windows and traditional architectural details.
description_view_0: This view shows the long side of the main wing and the central hub with the cupola and entrance portico clearly visible.
description_view_1: From this angle, the central hub and the shorter wings are prominent, with the cupola and chimney standing out.
description_view_2: This side highlights the opposite long wing and the central section, with the entrance portico and cupola visible from a different perspective.
description_view_3: The rear of the building is shown here, with the long wing extending away and the central hub and roof details visible.
